title: Rules
description: Hand-authored rule registry for a Workflow Analyzer rule package. One file per package, lives at registry/<PackageName>/rules.yaml. Consumed by the delivery-package generator; never shipped in a nupkg directly.

package.description: C# project name of the source library, e.g. 'Cpmf.WorkflowAnalyzerRules'.

namespace.description: Root namespace for rule classes, e.g. 'Cpmf.WorkflowAnalyzerRules.Rules'.

categories.description: Category definitions declared by this package. The codes appear as the category segment in rule IDs.

rules.description: All rules implemented in the source library.

RegistryRule.description: A rule entry in the hand-authored registry. Extends the rule schema with implementation fields needed by the generator.

id.description: Rule identifier in hyphen form, e.g. CPRIMA-T-001 or F401. This is the value passed to the Rule<T> constructor.

className.description: C# class name of the rule implementation, e.g. 'NoFlowchartStateMachineRule'. Used by the generator to locate the source file without scanning.

description.description: One-line description of what the rule checks.

recommendation.description: Actionable guidance shown to the user on violation.

categoryCode.description: Category code, e.g. T. Must match a code declared in this registry's categories array.

scope.description: SDK model type the rule inspects.

type.description: SDK type. Counters have no severity.

defaultSeverity.description: Default error level. Null for Counter (always Info).

defaultIsEnabled.description: Whether the rule is enabled by default in Studio.

requiredFeature.description: DesignFeatureKeys constant required to register this rule, or null. The generator emits a feature guard when this is set.

documentationLink.description: URL to rule documentation, or null.

tags.description: Arbitrary labels for filtering and grouping.

parameters.description: Configurable parameters exposed in Studio. Keys must be stable — they appear in pack manifests and generated code.

key.description: Parameter key used in code, e.g. 'Prefix'. Must be stable across versions.
